channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliament discusses Iranian soccer players’ asylum and fuel security measures
item.description: During today’s sitting in the House of Representatives, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke outlined the swift process through which humanitarian visas were granted to the Iranian women's soccer team, wh...

item.title: NLP Leader Calls for Transparent Audit of Nairobi Drainage Systems After Deadly Floods
item.description: Following the recent floods in Nairobi that claimed 26 lives and displaced around 800 households, National Liberal Party leader Dr. Augustus Muli is calling for a full public audit of the city’s drain...

item.title: Nairobi Medical Director Faces Court Over Alleged KSh11 Million SHA Fraud
item.description: A Nairobi-based medical director, Mishael Otieno Okumu, has been taken to court over allegations of orchestrating fraudulent claims exceeding KSh11 million from the Social Health Authority (SHA). Acco...

item.title: Security Firms Advised to Employ Only Trained Guards in Dodoma
item.description: Dodoma region security companies have been advised strongly to avoid hiring untrained guards just to save on salary costs. Wallace Lussingu, the new chairperson of the Tanzania Security Industry Assoc...

item.title: Acting Police Minister Says N2 Wall Won’t Replace Policing or Reduce Crime
item.description: Acting police minister Firoz Cachalia has stated that the proposed R120 million wall along the N2 in Cape Town is unlikely to reduce crime. The statement came in response to a parliamentary question f...

item.title: UK Political Figures Show Divergent Views on Involvement in Iran Conflict
item.description: The article reviews the contrasting positions of UK politicians regarding the US-led conflict with Iran. Labour leader Keir Starmer has opted for caution, advocating limited involvement due to the que...

item.title: Protests Erupt in Quezon City as Major Fuel Price Increases Take Effect Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: On March 10, 2026, various groups, including the peasant organisation Anakpawis and allied activists, staged demonstrations in Quezon City and other areas to protest a substantial oil price hike imple...

item.title: Massive Displacement in Lebanon Amid Israeli-Hezbollah Conflict
item.description: The ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah has led to a humanitarian crisis in Lebanon, displacing nearly 700,000 people, including around 200,000 children, according to UNICEF. The conflict es...

item.title: NBA Calls for Reform to Improve Legal Education and Training in Nigeria
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) don raise alarm say legal training for Nigeria need serious and structured transformation. Dis call come because of backlog wahala, funding shortage, and other chall...

item.title: Iran pursues prolonged conflict strategy against US and Israel through energy disruptions
item.description: Iran is taking a strategy focused on endurance rather than direct military confrontation to outlast the United States and Israel. The country, under the direction of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards C...

item.title: Pakistan and India clash at UN Security Council over terrorism and border security in Afghanistan
item.description: On Monday at the UN Security Council, Pakistan and India engaged in a tense exchange regarding Afghanistan. Pakistan’s Ambassador Asim Iftikhar Ahmad defended Islamabad’s counter-terrorism measures, e...

item.title: Australia faces strategic risks under AUKUS and should consider alternative defence plans
item.description: In a world marked by increasing instability, the AUKUS defence pact is raising significant concerns for Australia. While Prime Minister Anthony Albanese supports the initiative as central to national ...

item.title: Family of Tumbler Ridge Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI Alleging AI Assistance in Attack
item.description: The parents of Maya Gebala, a girl critically injured in the tragic mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., have initiated a civil lawsuit against artificial intelligence company OpenAI. The lawsuit, fi...

item.title: JEI Praises Tinubu’s Nationwide Climate Awareness Initiative
item.description: The Jewel Environmental Initiative (JEI), one prominent NGO, don commend President Bola Tinubu as e launch the Renewed Hope Climate Change Awareness Tour. According to Mr Ismail Bima, CEO of JEI, this...

item.title: Women of Fuji Documentary Premiered in Lagos to Celebrate International Women’s Day
item.description: A United States-based Nigerian professor and filmmaker, Saheed Aderinto, has premiered the second episode of his Fuji documentary series titled ‘Women of Fuji’ in Lagos. The event happened on March 8,...

item.title: Kwimba District Council Disburses Interest-Free Loans of 246 Million Shillings to 11 Women, Youth and Disabled Groups
item.description: Kwimba District Council in Mwanza Region has given out interest-free loans amounting to 246 million shillings to 11 groups made up of women, youth, and people with disabilities. This move is meant to ...

item.title: Interpol's Efforts to Identify Unnamed Female Murder Victims Across Europe
item.description: Interpol's specialised DNA unit is working to identify female murder victims across Europe whose identities have remained unknown for decades. Cases like that of Rita Roberts, found in Antwerp in 1992...

item.title: The Continuing Underrepresentation of Black Journalists in UK Newsrooms
item.description: Despite ongoing discussions about diversity in British journalism, Black journalists remain severely underrepresented in both newsroom staffing and recognition. A recent report by the Sir Lenny Henry ...

item.title: Four youths die in head-on motorcycle collision at Nedumangad
item.description: On the night of Sunday, March 9, 2026, a tragic road accident occurred at Nedumangad in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, leading to the death of four young men. The mishap took place around 10:45 ...

item.title: Government assures public: Supply is stable, avoid panic buying
item.description: The Presidential Palace has reassured the public that there is no need to rush into panic buying. Government officials emphasized that essential goods and commodities remain sufficiently available in ...
